title: 2017-08-23 | SB 3.26.22 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: The pure status of consciousness, or Krishna consciousness, exists in the beginning.
Just after creation, consciousness is not polluted.
The more one becomes materially contaminated, however, the more consciousness becomes obscured.
In pure consciousness, one can perceive the existence of the consciousness.
One can perceive a slight reflection of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
As in clear, unagitated water, free from impurities, one can see everything clearly.
So in pure consciousness, or Krishna consciousness, one can see things as they are.
One can see the reflection of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and one can see his own existence as well.
This state of consciousness is very pleasing, transparent.
In the beginning, consciousness is pure.
We are discussing from the chapter titled, Fundamental Principles of Material Nature.
It is interesting to see, in the Fundamental Principles of Material Nature, the verse is talking about consciousness.
We all know consciousness is spiritual, indestructible.
All pervading in the body, and doesn't end when the body ends.
And here, the vrutibhir lakshanam, the characteristic traits of pure consciousness, are explained.
So, when the consciousness is pure, it is evident in three characteristics.
One is, svacchatvam, it is pure, it is clear, clarity, transparent.
Avikaritvam means, there is no change.
Prabhupada translates this as, free from distractions.
If you want to understand distraction, then keep a mobile with WhatsApp.
Then, you will clearly understand what distraction means.
So, if you can be free from distraction, you have attained a pure consciousness.
So, you can see, it's a long journey to really come to the stage of freedom from distractions.
It's a long journey. I'll come to that.
Many people say, I don't have peace of mind, but actually, peace is connected to consciousness.
So, these are the three things.
Then, no change, or freedom from distraction.
These three are the vrittibhir lakshanam proktam.
The Shastra is declaring that these are the three characteristics of pure consciousness.
